sets.passage: My little sister Lily had wanted to ride a bicycle for months, so one Saturday I (1)___ to teach her. She was excited but also a little (2)___, gripping the handlebars far too tightly. 'The bike goes,' I said, 'where your eyes go.' Something (7)___. The next time she pushed off, she kept her head up, and for a few wonderful seconds she was actually (8)___ on her own. Her face filled with pure (9)___. She fell again, of course, but now each fall only made her more (10)___ to try again. By sunset she was riding shaky circles around the park, laughing (11)___. Watching her, I understood that you cannot give someone (12)___; you can only help them find their own. The best thing I did was not to hold the bike but to change where she was (13)___.
